warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Woodhaven Blvd from Union Turnpike to 107 Av. Est. Construction: 2026. Community Boards: CB5, CB6, CB9, CB10.
Woodhaven Blvd from Yellowstone Blvd to 77 Av. Est. Construction: 2028. Community Boards: CB5, CB6.
Woodhaven Blvd from Queens Blvd to 66 Av. Est. Construction: TBD. Community Boards: CB5, CB6.

6.85 miles of bus lanes installed on Woodhaven Blvd as part of Q52/Q53 SBS launch in 2017. Included offset, median, and curbside alignments; bus boarding islands at 12 bus stops; bus stop amenities; pedestrian safety measures at 14 intersections; refurbished medians with ADA compliant pedestrian refuges at 11 intersections.
Major bus corridor hosting the Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service, with 30,000+ daily riders, Vision Zero Priority Corridor since 2019

payload.claim_text: Woodhaven Boulevard is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor since 2019, with 3 Vision Zero Priority Intersections at Myrtle Av, Jamaica Av, and Rockaway Blvd
payload.subject: Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.claim_text: Woodhaven Blvd corridor has connections to 8 subway lines and 20 Local and Express bus routes
payload.subject: Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.claim_text: Challenges remain on Woodhaven Blvd: pedestrian safety measures don't have full impact until solidified with permanent materials; crowding and lack of amenities at bus stops; large sections of roadway and many medians remain in poor condition; some bus operations issues remain
payload.subject: Woodhaven Boulevard

payload.claim_text: Capital goals for Woodhaven Blvd: build out temporary pedestrian safety measures with permanent materials; install additional pedestrian safety measures; create more pedestrian circulation space and add amenities at bus stops; add trees and landscape elements throughout corridor
payload.subject: Woodhaven Boulevard
